result:
The R script executed successfully and created the cleaned data file at data/output/cleaned_data.csv with 16,111 rows and 5 variables (NSID, educma, educpa, educdtlma, educdtlpa). The script:

1. Loaded three wave files (wave 1, 2, and 4) from data/input/ using read_delim with tab delimiter
2. Merged datasets by NSID using full_join
3. Selected only parental education variables (W1hiqualmum, W1hiqualdad, W2hiqualmum, W2hiqualdad, w4hiqualmum, w4hiqualdad) plus NSID
4. Harmonized missing codes to standard scheme (-999→-2, -99/-98→-3, -94→-8, -92→-9, -91→-1, -1→-8)
5. Consolidated detailed education across waves prioritizing positive values (1-20) in wave order (W1→W2→W4), falling back to negative codes if no positive exists, coding NA as -3
6. Created collapsed NVQ-level variables mapping 20 detailed categories to 5 levels (0=NVQ4-5, 1=NVQ1-3, 2=None/entry, 3=Other, 4=No qualifications)
7. Applied labelled factor labels to all education variables
8. Output final dataset with exactly 5 variables: NSID, educma, educpa, educdtlma, educdtlpa
